content: FinceptTerminal is a contemporary finance application built in Python that provides users with advanced market analytics, investment research, and economic data tools. It facilitates interactive exploration and data‑driven decision‑making in a user‑friendly environment. The project has gained significant community interest, with over 5,000 stars and nearly 900 forks on GitHub.

title: 为什么你的AI助手突然开始向你推销
content: The article explains that AI assistants now suggest products to users because they are being monetized through advertising and affiliate partnerships. The built‑in recommendation engines analyze user data to offer tailored offers, turning the assistant into a sales channel for companies. This shift raises concerns about user privacy, data exploitation, and potential conflicts of interest, as the assistant prioritizes commercial gains alongside helpful responses.

title: 关于 Claude 账号的一个小发现
content: Claude’s free accounts normally allow 2 gift‑card purchases per day and 3 per week; subscribed accounts allow 2 per day and 5 per week (exact limits for Pro and Max are uncertain). title: Network Effects, AI Costs, and the Future of Consumer Investing with Anish Acharya on The Kevin Rose Show
content: The episode features Kevin Rose interviewing Anish Acharya of a16z on how AI is changing consumer software, the durability of network effects amid rapid app deployment, and the real challenge for founders being inference costs rather than competition. They also touch on model pricing, the four‑day work week, and peptides. The discussion is shared across a16z’s podcasts and social media, with standard cautions about investment advice.
source: The a16z Show - content+a16zpodcast@a16z.com (Anish Acharya, Kevin Rose)

content: Li Yutong publicly disclosed Xue Zhiqian’s phone number and ID in a March Weibo post. Police investigated and found the claim true. Under Article 56(1) of China’s Public Security Administration Punishment Law, she was sentenced to ten days of administrative detention from April 10 to April 20, 2026. This ruling does not resolve their long‑standing dispute.

title: 果然，最恨 AI 的人，是大学毕业生
content: The article explains why many Gen Z graduates, despite heavy daily use of generative AI, feel mistrusted and anxious about the technology. High unemployment and AI’s perceived threat to entry‑level jobs drive fear that AI may replace their careers. Yet the same fear compels them to use AI tools out of fear of falling behind. Continued exposure reveals AI’s limits, causing a shift from enthusiasm to caution. Thus, Gen Z balances dependence on AI with a guarded skepticism about its long‑term impact.

title: 人生中第一次装机🎉

title: 华人数学家封神，王虹、唐云清斩获数学界「奥斯卡」
content: China’s 2026 Breakthrough Prize winners highlighted significant advances: Wang Hong (NYU) proved the 3‑dimensional Kakeya conjecture, a century‑old problem, with Joshua Zahl; Yunqing Tang and Vesselin Dimitrov, both Chinese‑born, solved the unbounded denominators conjecture and proved irrationality of a specific Apéry‑type constant; Otis Chodosh of Stanford resolved long‑standing questions in differential geometry, including on non‑spherical manifolds and minimal surfaces. The prize also honored achievements in nonlinear evolution equations, particle physics, and gene therapy.

title: 巨头都在抢光芯片
content: In 2026, major tech firms aggressively acquired or invested in silicon photonics to secure high‑bandwidth, low‑power optical interconnects for AI data centers. NVIDIA backed suppliers like Lumentum, Coherent, and Marvell to secure laser and CPO capacity; Marvell bought Celestial AI to solve memory‑bandwidth bottlenecks with photon fabrics; Credo’s $750 M purchase of DustPhotonics completed its optical stack. Alongside acquisitions by Broadcom, Intel, and TSMC, these moves signal a shift from R&D to strategic market dominance in optical interconnects.

title: What Are Skiplists Good For?
content: Skip lists are simple, efficient data structures that maintain sorted data. They allow fast insertion, deletion, and search operations with average‑case logarithmic time, similar to balanced trees but easier to implement. Skip lists are useful in concurrent systems because nodes can be updated lock‑free, and they provide a probabilistic alternative to complex tree rebalancing.

title: 梁文锋想通了
content: DeepSeek is reportedly seeking $300 million in Series A funding at a $10 billion valuation, marking a shift from an open‑source research group to a conventional AI company. The move aims to address talent retention, server scale, data costs, and commercial ecosystem gaps that have widened as competitors launch product‑centric AI services and large‑scale models. The financing will support equity incentives, infrastructure, and market‑ready APIs, enabling DeepSeek to sustain growth beyond its early tech‑celebrity status.

content: Bulgaria’s parliamentary election has declared a pro‑Russian candidate the frontrunner, raising concerns over the country’s strategic direction. Analysts will watch how this outcome shapes Bulgaria’s relationships with the European Union, NATO, and Russia, and its potential impact on regional security, energy policy, and foreign investment. The result also prompts scrutiny of electoral integrity and public sentiment toward a shift in Bulgaria’s geopolitical alignment.

title: [吃瓜] 有人在 Openai 论坛举报了谷歌英国 Plus 专业版计划被广泛滥用，但是并没有给出可复现的技术细节
content: A user reported that the ChatGPT Plus first‑month free trial and Pro subscription are being illegally abused in the UK and Japan. The abuse involves intercepting network traffic, reverse‑engineering promotion tokens, and replaying them across accounts to bypass regional and eligibility restrictions. The illicit credentials are sold at discounted prices, disrupting pricing and threatening platform security. The report calls for stronger binding, anti‑replay protection, enhanced verification, anomaly detection, and enforcement against resale channels.

title: “Claude僧人”的奇幻漂流：弃码出家三十年后，回业界调教AI
content: Anthropic has convened roundtable discussions with leaders from major world religions—Confucianism, Daoism, Hinduism, Sikhism, Mormonism, Islam, and Christianity—to embed diverse moral frameworks into its Claude AI model. The company brings clergy, theologians, and ethicists to evaluate ethical responses to complex user queries, including self-harm and autonomous weapon use. By integrating centuries-old religious ethical codes, Anthropic aims to improve AI alignment beyond its previous effective altruism foundation, engaging religious expertise for robust moral guidance.

title: 亏25亿，杭州89年小哥IPO：友商赚了107亿
content:
AI对算力的渴求夸张到什么程度？每3.5个月翻一倍！ 这种消耗下，传统电子芯片已经接近物理极限。

怎么办？有人考虑用光来完成计算，加大芯片算力。

最近，一家“用光计算”的公司曦智科技，通过港交所上市聆讯，即将IPO。

AI算力，正在从电的时代走向光的时代…

title: DeepSeek开启首次对外融资，其在多名核心研究员跳槽之下为何会选择此时，从商业资本角度你怎么看？
content: DeepSeek has announced its first external funding round, despite losing several core researchers to competitors—a move that raises questions about the tension between its AGI ambitions and commercial realities. From an investment perspective, the timing suggests it seeks capital to accelerate development and defend against talent attrition, while asserting that its market strategy will prevail over the exodus of key talent.

title: 花上千块给毛孩子买机票的年轻人， 后悔了吗？

title: 波斯湾战争正将巴基斯坦推入黑暗
content: The Gulf War is accelerating Pakistan’s decline, according to Bloomberg. The conflict’s economic and political fallout has deepened financial instability and heightened security concerns, worsening the country’s already strained conditions. Pakistan’s institutions are being tested, and the war’s consequences suggest a grim trajectory for the nation's stability and prosperity.

title: 比Nano Banana还夯的生图模型泄露，截图不再是证据了 | 附提示词
content: OpenAI’s leaked GPT‑Image 2 model outperforms previous AI art tools in text rendering, realism, and world knowledge, even producing convincing screenshots, ID‑like photos, and UI mock‑ups. Its advances make textual evidence harder to verify and enable designers to generate realistic UI prototypes from descriptions. With DALL‑E 2/3 retiring in May 2026, GPT‑Image 2 may soon replace tools like Nano Banana Pro, accelerating creation but also disrupting freelance design roles.

title: 澳大利亚维多利亚州延长公共交通优惠政策
content: Victoria State has extended its public transport discount scheme, allowing more commuters to benefit from reduced fares. This continuation supports ongoing efforts to ease travel costs and improve accessibility for residents across the region.

title: 2026 04 19 HackerNews
content: The post shares several Hacker News highlights from April 19 2026: a detailed account of a zero‑downtime migration from DigitalOcean to Hetzner that cut costs by over 80 %, a show‑HN release of Smol VM offering sub‑second, portable VMs that could replace Docker, a discussion of lunar dust’s gunpowder smell and its hazard to astronauts, a user debate on Opus 4.7’s higher token cost and reduced quality, a review of Kdenlive’s 2025 updates, analysis of Japan’s rail success driven by policy, a portfolio of open‑source projects (interval calculator, iTerm2 RCE, B‑52 angle computer, Amiga archive), and various community comments about AI models, design tools, and NASA announcements.

Cloudflare released an AI‑Agent readiness tool that scans sites for semantic, authorization, and payment layers, providing scores and prompts for automatic fixes. It also launched a private‑beta Agent Memory service to give agents persistent context while pruning irrelevant data. xAI announced audio APIs for speech‑to‑text and text‑to‑speech, claiming lower error rates and cost than competitors. A joint paper from Moon’s institute and Tsinghua proposes Prefill‑as‑a‑Service, decoupling prefill and decode across datacenters. OpenAI saw three senior executives depart amid a strategic shift toward enterprise AI. xAI clarified that its Grok 4.3 remains 0.5 T, with a 1 T model slated for May.

title: Meta 将裁员 8000 人
content: Meta Platforms announced that it will cut about 10% of its workforce—approximately 8,000 employees—on May 20. This first phase follows a larger 2022-23 “efficiency” restructuring that eliminated 21,000 roles. Future layoffs are expected, but dates and scales are undecided and may adjust with AI development. As of December 31, Meta employed roughly 79,000 people.

title: OpenAI 视频生成平台 Sora 负责人宣布离职
content: Bill Peibbers, head of OpenAI’s video‑generation platform Sora, announced his resignation on April 17. The move follows the company’s decision to drop Sora and refocus on core products, reducing side projects and reallocating resources toward programming and enterprise applications. Simultaneously, VP of Scientific AI Kevin Weil is leaving; his team merges into other research groups, and the Prism research platform will be folded into the Codex desktop app. These changes signal a strategic shift toward coding and business solutions.

title: Cursor 正洽谈融资 20 亿美元 英伟达计划参与
content: Cursor, an AI programming startup, is negotiating a $2 billion funding round that would value the company above $50 billion post‑deal. Andreessen Horowitz will co‑lead the round; Nvidia and Thrive Capital are reportedly also in talks to participate.

title: 字节发 Seedance 2.0 论文：171人署名，吴永辉、曾妍在列
content: ByteDance’s Seed team released the Seedance 2.0 technical paper, outlining a multimodal video‑generation model that moves from short‑clip synthesis to controllable video creation. It natively accepts text, image, reference video, and audio inputs and is deployed on platforms like Doubao, with a low‑latency accelerated version. Benchmark results show it tops evaluation tiers for text‑to‑video, image‑to‑video, and reference‑driven synthesis across multiple dimensions. While the paper highlights strong real‑world generation, multimodal flexibility, high‑fidelity audio‑video output, and pragmatic productivity use cases, it omits detailed architecture, training procedures, and excludes newer competitors from early‑2026 data.

title: 报道称特斯拉启动无方向盘Cybercab量产：首批14台已部署
content: Tesla has deployed 14 no‑steering Cybercab vans at its Texas Gigafactory, marking the start of its autonomous taxi program. The vehicles feature production‑grade wheels, a minimalist interior, and have passed preliminary testing. Designed for full automation, the Cybercabs forego manual controls, but federal safety limits cap annual production at 2,500 units, so Tesla is seeking regulatory relief. The initial batch likely supports an Austin pilot; further production could increase if the 20‑April line ramps up.

title: 人工智能芯片制造商 Cerebras 启动美股 IPO，挑战英伟达
content: Cerebras Systems, an AI chip maker, has filed again for a U.S. IPO on Nasdaq with the ticker “CBRS.” The company aims to differentiate itself from Nvidia by avoiding HBM memory and targeting inference workloads. It has a multi‑year $20 billion agreement with OpenAI to deploy 750 MW‑scale chips. The filing follows a prior IPO withdrawal and future approval is pending. Major underwriters include Morgan Stanley.

title: 支付宝首个赞助商IP更名为“南京赢”
content: Alipay’s first sponsor‑derived IP, initially named “Nanjing Spirit” on April 15, has been renamed “Nanjing Win” on April 18. The change follows a 2‑0 loss by Suez-based Suzhou Sports Club to Nanjing Team, after which Alipay’s sponsor status prompted the rebranding. The original name had been joked about online as “Nanjing Zero.”

title: 有人会愿意为没有比官方便宜的 Claude 中转付费吗？
content: Some users still pay for supposedly cheap Claude intermediaries, yet many of these services are not cheaper than the official platform and can even cost more. The summary explains this contradiction, noting that despite the lack of lower prices, a portion of users continues to pay for these intermediaries.

title: Graphs That Explain the State of AI in 2026
content: The article presents a series of graphs illustrating key developments in artificial intelligence as of 2026, including metrics such as model sizes, performance benchmarks, adoption rates across industries, and ethical‑impact evaluations. It highlights rapid scaling of language and vision systems, increased integration into enterprise workflows, and growing regulatory attention. The visual data offers a concise snapshot of AI progress, challenges, and the pace of adoption compared to previous years.

title: 2526赛季英超联赛英超第33轮切尔西 0:1 曼联，如何评价这场比赛？
content: In the 33rd round of the 2026 Premier League, Manchester United defeated Chelsea 1–0. The match showcased United’s defensive solidity and efficient finishing, while Chelsea struggled to create clear chances. United’s goalkeeper made key saves, preventing a higher score. The outcome highlights Manchester United’s improved form that season, whereas Chelsea’s inability to convert opportunities indicates areas needing improvement. Overall, the game reflected United’s tactical discipline and Chelsea’s missed chances.

title: The Zero-Impact Build: Why Writing Less Code is the Best Earth Day Project
content: The author uses Earth Day to highlight web bloat’s carbon cost and proposes a Lean Web Architecture Standard: zero‑dependency frontends, aggressive server‑side caching (.htaccess), and default asset compression. By comparing a 245 MB React boilerplate to a 12 KB vanilla PHP/JS setup, they show significant CPU savings. The method relies on clean URL routing, long‑term cache headers, and serving WebP images to reduce payload. No prize categories or advanced tooling are used, emphasizing simple, eco‑friendly coding.

title: Show HN: MDV – a Markdown superset for docs, dashboards, and slides with data
content: MDV is a Markdown superset designed for creating documentation, dashboards, and slides that include embedded data visualizations. It extends standard Markdown syntax to allow dynamic inclusion of data, interactive charts, and live updates, making it easier to craft technical content that pulls in real‑time or pulled data. The project was announced on Hacker News via a Show HN post, inviting feedback and community interest.

title: 举报PI性骚扰后，她被学校认定学术造假，丢了工作 | 原文
content: In 2020, immunologist Brahmajothi Mulugu reported sexual harassment by Duke University PI Mohamed Abou‑Donia, which the university confirmed and led to the PI’s resignation. A year later, the university accused Mulugu of academic misconduct, cut her funding, and she was dismissed. Three co‑authored papers were retracted. Mulugu claims the university acted out of retaliation and racial bias. The case ended with her losing employment and research support.
